days.title: Working: the verb работя (I work)

grammar.title: Present tense of работя (e-type verb)
grammar.explanation:
Bulgarian has no infinitive. We name a verb by its **I**-form: **работя** means both 'to work' and 'I work'. To say who does the action, change the ending on the stem **работ-**:

- аз работ**я** (I work)
- ти работ**иш** (you work, informal)
- той/тя работ**и** (he/she works)
- ние работ**им** (we work)
- вие работ**ите** (you work, formal/plural)
- те работ**ят** (they work)

One Bulgarian present tense covers both English 'I work' and 'I am working'. Subject pronouns (аз, ти, той…) are optional because the ending already shows the person.

examples.english: I am working today.
They work a lot.
You work here.

reading.title: Къде работиш?
reading.text:
— Къде работиш днес?
— Работя вкъщи. А ти?
— Аз също работя много.
reading.translation:
— Where are you working today?
— I am working at home. And you?
— I also work a lot.

days.speakingPrompt: Say where you work and how much, using работя, вкъщи, офис, много and днес. Try at least three persons (аз, той, ние).

days.title: Reading: the verb чета (I read)

grammar.title: Present tense of чета (stressed-ending e-type)
grammar.explanation:
**чета** ('I read') uses the same person endings you met yesterday, but the stress and the linking vowel **-е-** fall on the ending, so the forms look a little different:

- аз чет**а** (I read)
- ти чет**еш** (you read)
- той/тя чет**е** (he/she reads)
- ние чет**ем** (we read)
- вие чет**ете** (you read)
- те чет**ат** (they read)

Notice the pattern across persons is always: -а, -еш, -е, -ем, -ете, -ат. As before, one present tense means both 'I read' and 'I am reading'.

examples.english: I am reading a book now.
He is reading a newspaper.
We read often.

reading.title: Какво четеш?
reading.text:
— Какво четеш сега?
— Чета една книга. Тя е хубава.
— Аз чета вестник всеки ден.
reading.translation:
— What are you reading now?
— I am reading a book. It is nice.
— I read a newspaper every day.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe what you read and how often, using чета, книга, вестник, често and бавно. Conjugate чета for аз, тя and ние.

days.title: Speaking: the verb говоря (I speak)

vocab.exampleEn: I am talking with a friend.

grammar.title: Present tense of говоря (и-type verb)
grammar.explanation:
**говоря** follows the same plan as **работя**: stem **говор-** plus the person endings -я, -иш, -и, -им, -ите, -ят:

- аз говор**я** (I speak)
- ти говор**иш** (you speak)
- той/тя говор**и** (he/she speaks)
- ние говор**им** (we speak)
- вие говор**ите** (you speak)
- те говор**ят** (they speak)

To say a language, just use it after the verb with no preposition: **говоря български** (I speak Bulgarian). Use **с** ('with') for the person you talk to: **говоря с приятел**.

examples.english: I speak a little Bulgarian.
She speaks English well.
They are talking with a friend.

reading.title: Говориш ли български?
reading.text:
— Говориш ли български?
— Да, говоря малко. Говоря и английски добре.
— Аз говоря с приятел всеки ден.
reading.translation:
— Do you speak Bulgarian?
— Yes, I speak a little. I also speak English well.
— I talk with a friend every day.

days.speakingPrompt: Say which languages you speak and how well, using говоря, български, английски, малко and добре. Add who you speak with using с + приятел.

days.title: Wanting: the verb искам (I want)

grammar.title: искам and the да-construction (no infinitive)
grammar.explanation:
**искам** is an a-type verb with endings -ам, -аш, -а, -аме, -ате, -ат:

- аз иск**ам**, ти иск**аш**, той/тя иск**а**, ние иск**аме**, вие иск**ате**, те иск**ат**.

Bulgarian has **no infinitive**. To say 'I want to read', use **да** + the present-tense verb, matching the same person: **искам да чета** (I want to read), **той иска да работи** (he wants to work). Both verbs agree with the subject.

examples.english: I want to read now.
He wants to work at home.
We want to rest.

reading.title: Какво искаш?
reading.text:
— Какво искаш сега?
— Искам кафе и малко вода.
— Аз искам да почивам вкъщи.
reading.translation:
— What do you want now?
— I want a coffee and a little water.
— I want to rest at home.

days.speakingPrompt: Say what you want, using искам with a noun (кафе, вода) and with да + verb (да чета, да почивам, да ям). Try аз, той and ние.

days.title: Doing and making: the verb правя (I do/make)

vocab.english: to do, to make, I do/make

vocab.exampleEn: In the morning I make coffee.

grammar.title: правя and making verbs negative with не
grammar.explanation:
**правя** is an и-type verb like говоря: прав**я**, прав**иш**, прав**и**, прав**им**, прав**ите**, прав**ят**. It means both 'do' and 'make'.

To make any present-tense verb negative, put **не** right before it: **не работя** (I don't work), **не чета** (I don't read), **той не иска** (he doesn't want). With a double-negative word like **нищо** ('nothing'), Bulgarian still uses **не** on the verb: **не правя нищо** (I'm not doing anything).

examples.english: What do you do in the evening?
In the morning I make dinner.
I am not doing anything now.

reading.title: Какво правиш вечер?
reading.text:
— Какво правиш вечер?
— Вечер чета книга. А сутрин правя кафе.
— Аз не правя нищо. Искам да почивам.
reading.translation:
— What do you do in the evening?
— In the evening I read a book. And in the morning I make coffee.
— I don't do anything. I want to rest.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe your morning and evening, using правя, вечеря, сутрин, вечер. Then say one thing you do NOT do, using не + a verb from this week.

days.title: A day of actions: putting the verbs together

vocab.english: to get up, I get up
to love, to like, I love/like

grammar.title: Linking actions: word order and connectors
grammar.explanation:
Bulgarian has **no noun cases**, so word order plus small connector words carry the meaning. Chain your daily verbs with **и** ('and'), and order them in time with **после** ('then'), **сутрин**, **вечер**:

- Сутрин ставам рано и правя кафе. После работя.

Like искам, the verb **обичам** takes **да** + present tense for an activity: **обичам да чета** (I love to read). Remember: every verb still agrees with its subject, and **не** before the verb makes it negative.

examples.russian: Сутрин ставам рано и правя кафе.
examples.english: In the morning I get up early and make coffee.

examples.russian: После работя и говоря с приятел.
examples.english: Then I work and talk with a friend.
In the evening I love to read a book.

reading.title: Един ден
reading.text: Сутрин ставам рано и правя кафе. После работя и чета. Вечер обичам да говоря с приятел.
reading.translation: In the morning I get up early and make coffee. Then I work and read. In the evening I love to talk with a friend.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe one whole day from morning to evening, linking at least four actions with и and после. Use ставам, работя, чета and обичам да….

days.title: Review: my daily actions

grammar.title: Recap: present tense of regular verbs
grammar.explanation:
This week you learned the Bulgarian present tense, which covers both 'I do' and 'I am doing'. Three ending patterns by verb type:

- **и-type** (работя, говоря, правя): -я, -иш, -и, -им, -ите, -ят
- **е-type** (чета): -а, -еш, -е, -ем, -ете, -ат
- **а-type** (искам, обичам, ставам): -ам, -аш, -а, -аме, -ате, -ат

Key points: Bulgarian has **no infinitive** — use **да** + present (искам да чета). Make verbs negative with **не** before the verb (не работя). Pronouns are optional. Bulgarian has no cases, so word order and prepositions (с, в, на) link the words.

examples.russian: Аз работя, чета и говоря български.
examples.english: I work, read and speak Bulgarian.

examples.russian: Той иска да почива, но не иска да работи.
examples.english: He wants to rest but doesn't want to work.
What do you do in the evening?

reading.title: Един обикновен ден
reading.text:
— Какво правиш сутрин?
— Ставам рано и правя кафе. После работя.
— А вечер?
— Вечер чета книга или говоря с приятел. Обичам да почивам.
reading.translation:
— What do you do in the morning?
— I get up early and make coffee. Then I work.
— And in the evening?
— In the evening I read a book or talk with a friend. I love to rest.

days.speakingPrompt: Tell the story of your typical day using all five focus verbs (работя, чета, говоря, искам, правя). Include one negative sentence with не and one искам да… or обичам да… sentence.
